claim_boundary: TICKET129 proves four exact intermediate results or route corrections. It proves or refutes none of RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.

riemann.core_definition: Let b(x)=exp(-1/(1-x^2)) for |x|<1 and b(x)=0 otherwise. Let C_Q be the finite sums of q*b((x-a)/r), where a and r>0 are rational and q is Gaussian rational.
riemann.proved_statement: C_Q is countable and dense in the LF test-function space C_c^infinity(R). Consequently {g*tilde(g): g in C_Q} is countable and dense, in the relative test-function topology, in the smooth compact-support autocorrelation image.
riemann.proof: Mollification by normalized rational-scale copies of b converges to each compactly supported smooth g in every derivative norm on one fixed enlarged compact set. Approximate each convolution integral and all derivatives through a chosen finite order by a Riemann sum, then approximate its complex coefficients and centers by Gaussian rationals and rationals. A diagonal sequence gives all derivative orders. Every approximant has a finite rational tuple description, so their union is countable. Convolution and reflection are continuous on a fixed support, hence g_n*tilde(g_n) converges to g*tilde(g).

finite_enumeration_audit.interpretation: Every row is finite and the nested union contains every finite rational bump description. Counts are descriptions, not quotient classes of equal functions.

riemann.connection_to_ticket128: Every core autocorrelation has compact support, so TICKET128 makes its prime-power side an exactly finite sum. This removes the previously open enumerability part without asserting positivity.

route_decision.retain: a countable dense core inside the autocorrelation image
route_decision.discard: density of the autocorrelation cone in the entire ambient test-function space, refuted by TICKET126

riemann.proof_boundary: The relative density theorem is exact. PrimeProject has not certified the archimedean term or nonnegativity of the Weil quadratic form on this core, so RH remains open.

collatz.proved_statement: If a least positive Collatz counterexample n exists, then n is odd and n>=2^28. For every 1<=j<=2^29, if a_i is the 2-adic valuation used by the ith accelerated odd step and S_j=sum a_i, then S_j<=ceil(j*log_2(3))=bit_length(3^j).
collatz.proof: Minimality gives x_i>=n for every accelerated odd iterate. Multiplying 2^a_i*x_(i+1)=3*x_i+1 yields 2^S_j=3^j*(n/x_j)*product_i(1+1/(3x_i)), hence 2^S_j<=3^j*(1+1/(3n))^j. Put n0=2^28 and m=3*n0. The binomial theorem gives (1+1/m)^m<sum 1/k!<87/32<11/4. For j<=2*n0, 3j<=2m, so (1+1/m)^(3j)<(11/4)^2<8 and (1+1/m)^j<2. Thus 2^S_j<2*3^j, which implies S_j<=bit_length(3^j).

route_decision.retain: use the exact prefix cap as a necessary-condition filter for changing-prefix searches
route_decision.discard: repeating the TICKET80 all-ones finite-prefix compactness argument or extrapolating TICKET128's finite scan

collatz.proof_boundary: This is a necessary condition for a hypothetical least counterexample, not a contradiction. Compatible valuation words still survive the finite cap, so Collatz remains open.

goldbach.proved_statement: Under the TICKET125/126 weighted Goldbach decomposition and the TICKET128 major coefficient floor A>1.31917, a uniform pointwise bound |R(N)|<=56*N/log(N) for every even N>4*10^18 is sufficient to glue the analytic tail to the verified finite range.
goldbach.proof: The identity log(H)=56 log(2)+18 log(5/4), together with positive atanh series and exact geometric tail bounds, gives 214/5<log(H)<43. Also H<2^62 and 1200^6<H, so the TICKET126 contamination coefficient satisfies B<21/10. Substitution into A-K/log(H)-B*log(H)^2/sqrt(H) leaves the displayed positive rational margin for K=56.

route_decision.retain: the exact finite-glue budget with rational certificates
route_decision.discard: K=55 as an unnecessarily strict sufficient target

goldbach.proof_boundary: The sufficiency constant is improved from 55 to 56, but the pointwise residual theorem itself is not proved. Strong Goldbach therefore remains open.

twin_prime.proved_statement: For positive cumulative K and Q_X=A_X/K_X, fix a left endpoint Y and q=Q_Y. For every X>=Y, Q_X-q=(Delta A-q*Delta K)/K_X. Therefore Q_X<=q+delta throughout a dyadic block if and only if Delta A-q*Delta K<=delta*K_X throughout that block.
twin_prime.proof: Substitute A_X=A_Y+Delta A and K_X=K_Y+Delta K into A_X/K_X-A_Y/K_Y and cancel A_Y*K_Y. The numerator becomes K_Y*(Delta A-q*Delta K), giving the identity and equivalence.
twin_prime.minimal_defect_function: D(Y)=sup_{Y<=X<=2Y} max(0,Delta A-Q_Y*Delta K)/K_X is the smallest admissible additive within-block delta at c=1.

strengthened_monotone_countermodel.statement: Endpoint ratio 0.92, monotonicity of both cumulative budgets, and exact denominator doubling still do not imply an all-scale ratio below one.

twin_prime.connection_to_ticket128: TICKET128 gives limsup q_j<=0.92. At c=1 the exact remaining coefficient theorem is limsup_j D(2^j)<0.08; equivalently, it suffices to prove an eventual uniform D(2^j)<=delta for some delta<0.08. Endpoint and monotonicity data cannot supply it.

route_decision.retain: estimate the signed synchronized increment Delta A-Q_Y*Delta K
route_decision.discard: endpoint interpolation from cumulative monotonicity or denominator growth alone

twin_prime.proof_boundary: The identity and countermodel are exact, but no asymptotic 0.08 defect bound is proved for the actual Vaughan coefficients. The parity barrier and exact gap-two positivity also remain.

literature_boundary.citation: Suzuki, Weil's quadratic form via the screw function
literature_boundary.role: Primary 2026 formulation of Weil positivity on C_c^infinity(R); TICKET129 proves only core density.

literature_boundary.citation: Tao, Almost all orbits of the Collatz map attain almost bounded values
literature_boundary.role: Primary almost-all boundary; the valuation cap is a universal-counterexample necessity, not convergence.

literature_boundary.citation: Oliveira e Silva, Herzog, and Pardi, Empirical verification of the even Goldbach conjecture
literature_boundary.role: Primary finite verification boundary H=4e18 used in the exact glue budget.

literature_boundary.citation: Ford and Maynard, On the theory of prime producing sieves
literature_boundary.role: Primary sieve boundary; the increment identity does not resolve parity or exact gap two.

enumerable_core_valuation_cap_endpoint_b.proof_boundary: TICKET129 proves four exact intermediate results or route corrections. It proves or refutes none of RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.
